tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Toddlers with sickle cell now have access to gene therapy that frees them from pain
stories.summary: CRISPR treatment approved for children as young as two; 100% of trial patients achieved freedom from severe pain crises.

stories.headline: Messi's embrace: 'Your people should be proud of you'
stories.summary: After Cape Verde's brave World Cup run, Lionel Messi embraced goalkeeper Vozinha with words of honor that will echo forever.

stories.headline: Twenty years of flags: One woman's quiet gift to her neighbors
stories.summary: Ruth Perdue has decorated over 100 neighborhood mailboxes with American flags every July 3 for two decades—pure patriotic delight.

stories.headline: Through tears and pain, Salah leads Egypt to historic World Cup victory
stories.summary: Mohamed Salah, 34, played through a hamstring strain to lead Egypt to their first-ever World Cup knockout win, defeating Australia 4-2 on penalties.

stories.headline: Community alerts lead rescuers to free juvenile humpback from crab pots
stories.summary: A juvenile humpback whale trapped under 800 pounds of crab pots in Alaska's Endicott Arm was freed after mariners' alerts sparked a multi-agency rescue.

stories.headline: Nerve implant lifts people from treatment-resistant depression; one in five become essentially depression-free
stories.summary: A surgically implanted vagus nerve device delivered lasting relief for treatment-resistant depression, with one in five patients achieving near-complete freedom.

stories.headline: Twenty-one-year-old Filipino phenom upsets defending Wimbledon champion
stories.summary: Twenty-one-year-old Filipino phenom Alex Eala upset defending champion Iga Swiatek 6-4, 3-6, 7-5 on Centre Court, marking her career-best Grand Slam performance.

stories.headline: Maine's piping plovers hit record high: hope for an endangered species
stories.summary: Maine's piping plovers hit a record 186 breeding pairs this year, beating last year's previous record by a dozen pairs.

stories.headline: Scientists create first fully synthetic cell that feeds, grows, and divides
stories.summary: SpudCell marks a breakthrough: a chemically-defined synthetic organism completing its full life cycle, opening doors to custom medicine.
